items.text:
Subject: Overdue Books — Please Return by July 10

Dear Mr. Okafor,

Our records show that two items borrowed from Hartwell Library are now overdue. "Modern Statistics" and "Coastal Ecology" were both due on June 25 and remain checked out under your account.

Please return these books to the circulation desk on the first floor of Hartwell Library by Friday, July 10. A late fee of $0.50 per book is added each day, and your account currently owes $7.00. Should the balance reach $20.00, your account access will be temporarily blocked until the fees are paid.

If you have already returned the books, or if you would like to renew them for your summer research, please reply to this message or call the desk at 555-0142 before the deadline. Renewals are not available for items that other students have requested.

The library is open from 8:00 AM to 9:00 PM on weekdays. Thank you for your prompt attention.

Best regards,
Ms. Priya Nadar
Circulation Supervisor, Hartwell Library

format_metadata.subject: Overdue Books — Please Return by July 10

questions.stem: According to the email, what will happen if Mr. Okafor's account balance reaches $20.00?

options.A: A renewal will be granted automatically.
options.B: His account access will be temporarily blocked.
options.C: A replacement copy will be ordered.
options.D: His research materials will be reshelved.

questions.explanation: The email states that should the balance reach $20.00, the account access will be temporarily blocked until the fees are paid.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about renewing the two books?

options.A: Renewal requires a payment of $7.00 first.
options.B: Renewal must be arranged at another library branch.
options.C: Renewal depends on whether other students have requested them.
options.D: Renewal cancels the fees already charged to the account.

questions.explanation: The email says renewals are not available for items that other students have requested, so whether the books can be renewed depends on whether other students want them.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To announce new opening hours for the library.
options.B: To request immediate payment of a $20.00 fine.
options.C: To invite the student to a summer research workshop.
options.D: To remind the student to return two overdue books.

questions.explanation: The email's central message is that two borrowed books are overdue and must be returned by the deadline; the fees and hours are supporting details.

items.text:
Subject: Kitchen Faucet Repair — This Thursday Morning

Hi Daniel,

I wanted to let you know that the maintenance office is sending a technician to fix the leaking kitchen faucet in our apartment, Unit 12B, this Thursday, September 18. The technician is scheduled to arrive between 9:00 AM and 11:00 AM, and one of us needs to be home to let him in.

I have my chemistry lab until noon on Thursday, so could you stay until he finishes? If neither of us is there, the office charges a $25 missed-appointment fee, and I'd rather not waste that money.

Also, we're almost out of dish soap and paper towels. If you're heading to Greenview Market anyway, could you pick some up? I'll pay you back for my half when I get home. The building's water will be shut off from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM during the repair, so let's fill a few bottles the night before.

Thanks so much,
Maya

format_metadata.subject: Kitchen Faucet Repair — This Thursday Morning

questions.stem: According to the email, why does Maya ask Daniel to be home on Thursday morning?

options.A: To meet with the building manager.
options.B: To let the technician into the apartment.
options.C: To pay the maintenance office in person.
options.D: To shut off the building's water supply.

questions.explanation: Maya explains that someone needs to be home to let the technician in, and because she has a chemistry lab until noon, she asks Daniel to stay.

questions.stem: According to the email, what does Maya ask Daniel to buy at Greenview Market?
questions.explanation: The email states they are almost out of dish soap and paper towels and asks Daniel to pick some up if he goes to Greenview Market.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the apartment's water supply on Thursday?

options.A: It will stay running throughout the repair.
options.B: It will be unavailable for part of the morning.
options.C: It will be tested for safety after the repair.
options.D: It will require a fee to be restored.

questions.explanation: Because the water will be shut off from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM, the roommates will have no running water for part of the morning, which is why Maya suggests filling bottles the night before.
